description: SecID resolver conformance fixtures — canonical test cases that every implementation (SecID-Service Worker, SecID-Server-API Python/TS/Go) must satisfy. Behavioral assertions are the hard contract; strict assertions are optional pin-downs run with --strict.

tests.name: resolve-cve-2021-44228
tests.description: Canonical CVE record — Log4Shell. Tests the most-common advisory resolution path: namespace-qualified SecID with subpath ID maps to a primary URL plus aggregated alternate sources.

tests.name: resolve-cwe-79
tests.description: Canonical weakness resolution — XSS (CWE-79). Single primary URL, no alternate sources.

tests.name: resolve-attck-t1059-003
tests.description: ATT&CK technique with subpath that contains a dot (T1059.003 — PowerShell). Verifies dots in subpath IDs are preserved and not normalized away.

tests.name: resolve-ccm-iam-12
tests.description: Versioned control with no per-item URL — CCM @ v4.0 IAM-12. Verifies version handling AND description-only results: when no URL exists, the response carries a 'data' field with descriptive context. This is the canonical contract — 'data' for description-only results, no 'data' for URL-bearing results.

tests.name: resolve-cvss-40
tests.description: Versioned methodology (CVSS @ 4.0). Verifies version handling for methodologies and that the response includes contextual data for description-style entries.

tests.name: resolve-cve-cross-source
tests.description: Cross-source search — bare type with no namespace. Should aggregate matches from every advisory namespace that recognizes the CVE-2021-44228 pattern.

tests.name: resolve-aiid-1
tests.description: Incident subtype — AIID incident 1. Verifies the incident subtype tag (added 2026-05-27) is exposed AND that the resolution to incidentdatabase.ai works.

tests.name: resolve-not-found
tests.description: Unknown namespace — confirms 'not_found' status and 0 results. Tests the 'helpful over correct' contract: never an error, always an envelope.

tests.name: list-advisory-namespaces
tests.description: Bare type listing — secid:advisory should return descriptive metadata about the advisory type. Tests the type-level discovery path.

tests.name: list-types
tests.description: GET /api/v1/types — returns all 10 SecID types with their descriptions and subtypes. The canonical type-list endpoint.
